questions.question: What wildlife protection and habitat conservation regulations apply in this area, and how are they enforced?
questions.answer: The Town of Mt. Pleasant has a Conservation Advisory Council (CAC) that advises the Town Board on matters affecting the protection of the environment. The town is conducting a Natural Resource Inventory (NRI) that compiles information on key natural features including geology, soils, streams, wetlands, forests, and wildlife. The NRI includes maps, data, and reports describing these resources, and incorporates information on significant biodiversity areas, wetland, stream, and forest habitats, as well as rare plant and animal species. The CAC engages the public through public information sessions, site visits, and community input on environmental features. No specific statutory wildlife protection or habitat conservation regulations with defined enforcement mechanisms are identified in the provided sources.
